summary: Iteration 3 backend testing complete. 34/34 pytest tests pass (23 regression from iter2 + 11 new for Day Plans + AI Companion). Verified: GET /api/day-plans/{date} returns default empty plan when missing (no 404), PUT upsert + persistence + re-update, GET /api/day-plans desc-sorted list. Companion: GET creates default {Najm,friend,friend}, PUT partial updates preserve other fields, memories CRUD with desc sort, messages chronological asc + clear-without-touching-memories. Real Claude Sonnet 4.5 chat: reply > 50 chars (not fallback), persona persisted on every message, user_name 'Captain' addressed in reply, vegetarian memory injection respected (no meat suggested). No MongoDB _id leak across any of the new 8 endpoints.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~791 lines — has crossed the 700-line threshold. Strongly recommend splitting into modules: models.py, routes/ (workouts, recipes, journal, events, life_goals, day_plans, companion, ai), seed.py, lifespan.py before adding more features. Currently every new feature compounds the file.

POST /api/companion/chat swallows LLM exceptions and saves a hard-coded fallback assistant message ('I'm here. Let's try that again in a moment.') with no error indicator returned to the client. UI cannot distinguish a real reply from a fallback — consider returning HTTP 502 or a {fallback: true, error: ...} flag so the frontend can surface a retry affordance.

Companion chat history is loaded with .sort('created_at', -1).to_list(20) then reversed — fine for now, but with growing history this Python-side reverse + last-12 slice is wasteful; use .sort(..., 1).skip().limit() or aggregation. Also there is no per-conversation/session scoping — all messages are global, which means clearing wipes everything regardless of persona context.

DELETE /api/companion/memories/{id} and DELETE /api/companion/messages return 200 {ok:true} unconditionally even when nothing was deleted (no matched_count check). Same pattern as flagged in iter2 for other DELETE endpoints — please standardise to 404 on miss.

PUT /api/day-plans/{date} accepts the body's payload.date as-is and then forcibly overwrites it with the path date. Good defensively, but consider returning 400 if body.date != path date instead of silently overwriting (catches client bugs).

DayPlan model uses Field(default_factory=lambda: {...}) for meals — this works, but supplements/house_chores/work_chores are typed as List[dict] (untyped). Define inner Pydantic models (Supplement, ChoreItem, MealSlot) for validation; right now any client can store arbitrary keys.

Memory injection into the system prompt is unbounded by token count — caps at 30 memories but each memory could be long. With dozens of long memories + 12 history messages, the system prompt could blow past Claude context budget. Consider truncating each memory to 240 chars and tracking total token estimate.

PERSONA_PROMPTS dict is module-level constant — fine, but there's no validation on PUT /api/companion that persona is one of the 4 allowed values. A client could set persona='banana' and the chat would silently fall back to 'friend'. Add a Literal['friend','secretary','manager','coach'] to CompanionUpdate.persona.
Companion.id is hard-coded as 'default' — single-companion-per-deployment design. Fine for the current single-user app, but worth noting as a non-multi-tenant assumption.
seed_data_creation: No new seed. TestCompanion uses an autouse fixture that wipes companion messages + memories and resets the companion to defaults before each test, so iter3 tests are idempotent. TestDayPlans writes to far-future dates (2030-*) to avoid collision with real user data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py now has 34 tests. Run: pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v (~68s, dominated by 7 Claude Sonnet 4.5 calls). Companion tests reset state via autouse fixture, so they will wipe any companion messages/memories the user created during manual testing — warn the user or skip TestCompanion if preserving state matters. Day plan tests use future dates (2030-10-01, 2030-10-05, 2030-11-15, 2030-12-31) and do not delete after themselves; if the list endpoint cap (60) becomes an issue across many iterations, add cleanup.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All iteration 3 endpoints behave per spec: defaults on first GET, upsert semantics on PUT, no _id leak, persona/user_name/memory all flow into Claude system prompt and shape the reply correctly.
